target.name: Distributed Spectrum

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Distributed Spectrum is a Seed-stage defense and security company that has raised $25M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI—spanning the same sector and development stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $25M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $62M. Peer-median analysis examines four same-sector comparables at Seed or Series A stage: Tenna Systems at $60M, Ares Industries at $150M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Stark Defence at $500M. The median across these four peers is $225M, with a range of $60M to $500M. These peers represent both current-stage validation and near-term stage-advancement benchmarks.

Triangulating the two methods produces a valuation range of $53M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $144M. The 5x spread between low and high reflects material uncertainty regarding the company's path to Series A and relative positioning within the defense-tech peer set. Key sensitivities include the timing and terms of the next funding round, competitive positioning relative to Tenna Systems and Anduril Industries, and macro conditions affecting defense spending. The peer-median method carries greater weight given the larger sample, though stage-progression provides downside calibration.
